pbe_bench_lite.description: Programming by Example (PBE) String Transformations. Synthesis of Python str.replace() operations from input/output examples.

interpretation.failure_mode_addressed: Task 5 failed on control due to overlapping/multi-operation replacements (synthesizing three steps incorrectly instead of one compound change). Cold/warmed start passed via distilled context rule: 'when transformations interact, look for the minimal encompassing change'.
interpretation.latency_drop_reason: Avoiding the failure loop on Task 5 (which spent 1.53s and 2,943 tokens on wrong attempts) significantly lowered the mean latency.

slr_bench.description: Symbolic Logic Reasoning (SLR) Prolog Rule Induction. Inducing classification rules from positive/negative train examples.

interpretation.non_regression_proof: SLR tasks are solved perfectly (100.0%) by the base Qwen2.5-Coder model alone. LearnKit maintained this perfect score with zero regression.
interpretation.performance_tradeoff: A 37% token overhead is introduced by memory context injection, but it yields a 5% latency reduction due to structural pattern confirmation rather than re-deriving logic rules from scratch.

pbe_bench_lite_20260627.description: PBE-Lite rerun 2026-06-27 with Instruct-32B (not Coder). Included for completeness; do NOT compare directly with the 2026-06-03 Coder-32B numbers.
pbe_bench_lite_20260627.caveat: PBE synthesis tasks require the Coder model variant. Instruct-32B does not reliably generate str.replace() programs. Compare only Coder vs Coder.

slr_bench_20260627.interpretation: SLR is model-insensitive: Instruct-32B matches Coder-32B on control (100%). 2 failures in cold/warmed at tasks 2 and 4 (overgeneralization); contrastive failure records distilled. Non-regression confirmed: no arm scores below control.

agentic_synthesis_20260627.description: Agentic PBE/SLR (synthesis_agentic.py). Reframes both task shapes as a propose->execute->observe->refine tool loop so LearnKit's agent/procedural path (@lk.agent_learn: procedure replay + playbook guidance) engages, unlike the single-shot model-path runners above. Stream = exposure -> exact-repeat -> sibling per family. Win = warmed LLM calls <= cold while success held.
agentic_synthesis_20260627.metric: llm_calls (planning cost); lower is better. success held at 100%.
agentic_synthesis_20260627.interpretation: Converts the single-shot null/regression PBE/SLR result into a genuine procedural win: -33% planning cost (LLM calls) at 100% success on both task shapes and both 32B models (coder and general-instruct). Exact repeats hard-replay the learned procedure (0 LLM calls); siblings reuse it via injected guidance. Reproduce: python -m benchmarks.synthesis_agentic --kinds pbe slr.
